headline: Constitutional amendments require only simple majority to table
context: While constitutional amendments require two-thirds majority to pass, they can be tabled with simple majority, allowing the government to use constitutional change as political signalling.

cards.big: Constitutional amendments tabled as political statements — 4 failed deliberately in 2025
cards.sub: Government tables amendments knowing they'll fail, to brand opposition as obstructionist

cards.big: Article 159 requires two-thirds vote but sets no minimum consultation period before tabling
cards.sub: Constitutional changes can be introduced and debated within days of drafting

cards.big: 4 constitutional amendment bills in 2025 consumed 38 hours of parliamentary time
cards.sub: Doomed-to-fail bills used scarce legislative time that could address other priorities

cards.big: Ireland requires referendum for all constitutional amendments, mandatory public debate period
cards.sub: Serious constitutional change processes include built-in deliberation safeguards

cards.big: Tabling amendments you know will fail isn't governance — it's using the constitution as a campaign prop
Should constitutional amendments require minimum 90-day public consultation before tabling?
